subject: Project Management
title: Optimism Bias
description: A micro-lesson that makes Optimism Bias usable.

hook.text: 80% of people believe they are above-average drivers. The math doesn't work, but the brain insists.

buildup.text: Optimism bias makes us overestimate good outcomes and underestimate bad ones — in every domain.

discovery.text: In project planning, optimism bias means we assume fewer problems, faster progress, and smoother execution.

twist.text: Optimism bias is adaptive for survival — but terrible for scheduling. Evolution didn't design us for Gantt charts.

climax.text: The brain's built-in positivity filter is project management's most persistent hidden enemy.

punchline.text: Your brain is wired for hope, not for deadlines.

quiz.question: Why is optimism bias harmful in project planning?
options: We assume fewer problems and faster progress than reality
It only affects new project managers
